mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150k signals robust labor market demand, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings and lower equity risk premiums over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2015 payroll growth 142071k indicates sustained hiring momentum
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.50% after an extended period near zero, it signals the Fed's confidence in economic recovery and reduced tail risks, but simultaneously increases discount rates applied to future cash flows. The net effect on equity returns depends on whether earnings growth expectations rise faster than discount rates. This mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.50% in a tightening cycle.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.50% after 12+ months below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:July 2015 FEDFUNDS at 0.13% indicates near-zero rates still in effect
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.50% threshold, or Rank IC > -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, indicating late-cycle deceleration. When both series decline month-over-month simultaneously, equity investors reprrice earnings expectations downward, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:June 2015 RSAFS 446477M and HOUST 1174k show demand indicators
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of co-decline instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 12-month trend, signaling supply-side capacity expansion and productivity gains. This reduces unit costs and supports profit margins, driving positive 20-day equity returns through improved earnings visibility over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth rate exceeds 12-month average by more than 0.5 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:July 2015 INDPRO 107.477 reflects manufacturing activity levels
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of acceleration instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, creating negative real rates. This signals monetary accommodation that supports asset prices through financial conditions easing, producing positive 60-day forward returns as investors extend duration and equity risk appetite.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2015 CPIAUCSL 237.786 and July 2015 FEDFUNDS 0.13% show accommodative stance
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration and increased recession risk. Equity investors reprice tail risk upward and reduce equity allocations, producing negative 5-day forward returns as the signal propagates through markets.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3pp over any 3-month rolling window
evidence_refs: fact:July 2015 UNRATE 5.3% represents labor market tightness
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 50% of deterioration instances, or Rank IC > -0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) remain below 1.2 million units annualized, indicating weak aggregate demand and construction underperformance. This dual weakness signals late-cycle stagnation, producing negative 60-day forward returns as investors reduce growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND HOUST < 1200k units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2015 GDPC1 16270.4B and June 2015 HOUST 1174k show demand weakness
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 55% of co-weakness instances, or the interaction term Rank IC < 0.015 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises, creating stagflationary pressure. This signals supply-side constraints and reduced real economic capacity, producing negative 20-day forward returns as investors reprice earnings downward and demand higher equity risk premiums.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:July 2015 INDPRO 107.477 and June 2015 CPIAUCSL 237.786 provide supply-inflation data
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of stagflationary instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.
